confirmed.title: FILL MODEL: contrarian entry after >=12bps prior move — uncensored book ~20s in: p5 .47/p25 .505/p50 .525/p75 .555/p95 .595; real capped fills: p25 .47/p50 .51/p90 .55, share-wtd mean .486; ~69% of signals fillable <=55c
confirmed.method: Buffered open-to-open prior moves from cb5m joined to pm_prices_sample (49 signal intervals of 216) for the uncensored CLOB minute-point distribution of the contrarian side; ground truth from 155 live reversal-family ledger fills (real book-walk avg + 1c slip, entrySec p50=9s, censored at the 55c revEntryMax cap); t0-join of both (n=28) to measure ask-vs-minute-mid; spread from signals.log (n=1,482, median 1c, p95 2c). Scripts: work/microstructure/ledger_fill.py + thinbook_spread.py; tables fill_dist.json, signal_sample.json.
confirmed.effect: Backtest recipe: fill = ask+1c where ask at ~9s post-open runs ~2c BELOW the minute-mid (join median -0.020, mean -0.029, n=28) — i.e. entering fast is slightly cheaper than the p20 snapshot implies. Capped-at-55c fills: p5 .39/p10 .414/p25 .47/p50 .51/p75 .53/p90 .55/p95 .55, share-wtd mean .4861 (q*=.5036). Uncensored availability: 69.4% of signals price <=55c+slip at ~20s (the other ~31% sit .555-.60 and must be modeled as SKIPS, not 50c fills); live fillability 70/82 eligible intervals (85%). Larger prior moves price the contrarian side richer: c20 median .505 (12-16bps) vs .535 (>=16bps). By ~60s the contrarian mid disperses violently (p25 .295/p75 .605) — late entries inherit adverse selection.
confirmed.train: c20 median .53, avail .656, ledger entry median .51 (n=105)
confirmed.test: Chronological 2/3-1/3 split: fill quantiles stable (ledger entry median .51 both halves; c20 .53 vs .505; availability .66 vs .77). Block-bootstrap (1h) CIs in sigtests.json.
confirmed.feeAdjustedEdge: This is a COST INPUT, not an edge claim: at wtd fill .486 the hurdle is q*=.5036; the live family won 52.3% pooled (+1.9pp net, PnL +$345 = +$621 misprice - $276 fee), but the last-third win rate is .380 (n=50, block-boot CI .12-.62) — the fill distribution is TEST-stable, the reversal edge at these fills is not resolvable at this n.
confirmed.notes: Promising = the deliverable itself (fill distribution + availability) is robust and replicated out-of-sample; every reversal backtest should use these quantiles with a 31% skip rate above the 55c cap. Caveat: p20/p60 are prices-history minute buckets (nearest point within 90s), mid-ish not asks — hence the ledger anchoring. Paths: work/microstructure/fill_dist.json, thinbook_spread.json.

votes.reason: All headline numbers reproduce within tolerance (most exactly), and every adversarial attack failed to break the finding. (1) Look-ahead: re-fetched 12 sampled markets from the CLOB prices-history API — the point used as 'p20' is stamped t0+3..19s in all 12 cases, i.e. BEFORE the ~t0+20s decision time, and stored values equal refetch 12/12; the '20s' snapshot is really a ~5-19s price, consistent with live entrySec p50=9s, so no future information enters. (2) Boundary/overlap: my independent buffered open-to-open construction (consecutive-candle required) reproduces n and all quantiles exactly. (3) Threshold sensitivity: shifting the 12bps trigger +/-20% (9.6/14.4bps) leaves the fill distribution stable (c20 median .515/.525, availability .80/.725); the 55c cap at -20% mechanically collapses availability (~2%) because prices cluster near 50c, but the cap is the bot's pre-registered revEntryMax, not a data-fitted parameter, so this is not a snooping artifact. (4) Multiple comparisons: this is a descriptive cost-input (quantile estimation at pre-registered live-bot parameters), not a selected hypothesis test, and the finding itself explicitly disclaims the reversal edge (last-third win rate ~.38-.39, wide CI) — the only part a Bonferroni discount would hit is already flagged as unresolved. Residual caveats (do not refute): the ~2c fast-entry cheapness rests on n~30 with wide spread (treat as 1.5-3c, sign robust), and the 69-71% availability uses minute-bucket mid-ish prices rather than asks, but live fillability (85%) brackets it from above so modeling 31% skips is conservative. Full audit trail: /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-sgonzalez/4f584d6e-5c8f-4a76-8bd9-6dcf248c8bf8/scratchpad/work/verify-microstructure/ (reproduce.py, lookahead_check.py, repro_results.json, lookahead_check.json, VERDICT.json).

The fill-model deliverable is genuine and fee-honest: feeEntry matches shares*0.07*p*(1-p) exactly on all 155 trades, zero exit fees (hold-to-resolution, no hedge/stop legs), fillFrac=1.0 throughout, no entries above the 55c cap+slip. It is correctly framed as a COST INPUT, not an edge claim, and it errs conservative: backtests at c20+1c with >55c skips price worse than live fills (join-set EV/share -0.049 vs -0.009 actual) and modeled availability 69% < live fillability 85%. One correction survived my attack: the '-2c below minute-mid' fast-entry recipe is partly a cap-censoring artifact — restricting the join to the uncensored region (c20<=.54, n=23) gives median -1.5c/mean -2.0c, while the -2.9c headline mean is inflated by 5 mechanically-selected above-cap fills (mean -6.9c). Backtests should use ~-1.5c or the ledger entry quantiles directly. This adjusts a secondary parameter in the conservative direction and does not overturn the finding; the finding also correctly discloses that the reversal edge at these fills does not clear q* on TEST (last-third .38-.39 < .5036).

Core deliverable survives the adversarial re-splits: ledger fill distribution is stable across chronological thirds (entry median .51/.50/.50, wtd .491/.473/.494, n=51/51/53 — TEST fold n=53>=30) and across days (.482 vs .495), and the 55c-cap/1c-slip parameters behave consistently across folds (availability .56/.75/.77 brackets the pooled .694). Weekly split is impossible (pm sample spans 3 days, ledger 32h) — thirds and per-day used instead. Two components FAIL and must not be used as stated: (1) the '-2c cheaper fast entry' join adjustment is unstable (halves median -0.050 vs -0.015, n=28<30) — backtests should use the ledger entry quantiles directly, not mid-minus-2c; (2) regime coverage is badly skewed: only 5 of 49 pm signals are trending (Kaufman eff>0.48) while 23.3% of >=12bps signals over the full 60d cb5m fire in trending regimes, and the sparse trending data shows richer fills (c20 med .535 vs .525) and lower availability (.60 vs .705), matching the prior-move-size gradient (>=24bps: avail .60). The headline .525 median / 69% availability is calm-regime-fitted and would be optimistic in a strongly trending stretch — any backtest using this fill model on trending segments should haircut availability toward ~.60 and shift fills ~1-2c richer. Not refuted because the finding claims only a cost input (not an edge), its central quantiles replicate exactly, are TEST-stable at n>=30 on the ledger side, and it already caveats the 31% skip rate and win-rate non-resolvability. Confidence medium (not high) because all price data comes from a single 3-day window of one market week, so genuine out-of-sample regime diversity is untestable.

Strictly dominated by reducing stake fraction.
Daily trade caps: same pattern (N3: -57% EV / -24% DD on TEST at live cadence); the apparent full-60d benefit of caps comes entirely from truncating the -EV TRAIN regime, i.e. hindsight luck.

Adaptive allocation on the real ledger (trades.json incl. pre-reset): only 4 trading days of daily pnl across 10 engines — Hedge -$838 and Thompson -$1,657 vs best static +$202; nothing learnable at this history length.
Static equal-weight across arms: -$942 on TEST and -$4,855 over 60d — worse than every alternative except the momentum arm; diversification across known-bad engines is not protection.

Full Kelly at the verified design point (f*=6.9%): highest median TEST wealth but 79% median drawdown, 11.8% P(net loss), and near-certain wipeout (median terminal $1) under the TRAIN regime where realized q undershot the assumed 0.56 — unusable operating point given finding-2 regime drift.

Sizing off the live empirical fill distribution while holding q fixed at the measured value: makes even the fee-dead TRAIN regime profitable, but assumes win prob is independent of fill price (cheap fills = market disagrees more), which is unsupported; demoted to sensitivity only.

First live-cadence caps run (work/sizing/caps_livefreq.py): a single 10% thinning draw landed below break-even by chance and produced meaningless EV-cost percentages (>100%); superseded by per-rep re-thinning in caps_livefreq2.py.
rejectedTitles: The reversal edge is not stationary over 60d: fee-dead in the first 40 days, and the efficiency gate's benefit flips sign between TRAIN and TEST
Adaptive allocation (Thompson, Hedge) does NOT beat 100% latentfire — it is insurance against picking a bad arm, at ~30-37% of TEST pnl
Daily loss caps and trade caps are NOT EV-neutral variance tools here — EV cost runs ~2x the drawdown reduction at every level tested; dominated by staking less
Fill price is the dominant sizing lever: 2c of entry price moves EV more than any Kelly-fraction or cap decision
Fee-adjusted Kelly = 6.9% of bankroll; flat $50 is already ~0.73x full Kelly, and quarter-to-half Kelly ($17-$34 at $1k) dominates it on risk

dead: Ex-ante quiet-vol Up bias: P(Up) in bottom trailing-vol decile is 0.470 on TEST (0.514 TRAIN, boot p=0.17) — sign-flips out of sample; pooled D0-D2 TEST 0.490. The core tie-rule trade idea is dead.

Realized-flat Up bias as an edge: P(Up|realized |move|<1bps)=0.52 in Coinbase data, but (i) uses future information (realized flatness), and (ii) actual Polymarket resolutions in the same bucket run 0.508 vs proxy 0.556 — the bias is partly proxy artifact.
Price discreteness / exact ties: Coinbase c==o occurs in only 0.052% of 17,268 intervals; sub-0.25bps micro-moves resolve Up only 8/18 in actuals. No discreteness channel.

Buy Up at p20<=0.52 in forecast-vol bottom decile: n=0 tradable markets in the entire 3-day live-price sample (D0 regimes absent in the recent vol environment); relaxed D0-D2 version n=19 with PnL CI of +/-0.21/share — unpowered, and proxy TEST win rate 0.490 vs 0.5375 break-even says negative at scale.

Quiet-regime session interaction: TEST quiet-x-US P(Up)=0.535 (n=346) looks tempting but is post-hoc, fails the p<0.01 TRAIN bar (Asia/EU both ~0.47), and is exactly the multiple-testing noise the brief warns about.

Session-level flatness targeting: session differences in flat fraction are small (18.6%-22.0% <2bps) relative to the vol-decile gradient (7.6%-36.3%) — session adds nothing once trailing vol is conditioned on.
rejectedTitles: Flatness is strongly forecastable from trailing vol (structure, not edge)
No ex-ante Up bias in quiet regimes: P(Up) by trailing-vol decile is flat/negative on TEST
Realized-flat Up bias is largely a Coinbase-proxy artifact, not an oracle effect
Oracle-proxy noise concentrates below 1bps, not 2bps (methodological, for other agents)
Book prices quiet intervals fair; 'buy Up cheap when vol bottom-decile' is untestable-as-specified and negative by proxy

dead: Walk-forward trailing-10d threshold re-selection (forced and with abstain): -0.0044 and -0.0019/share vs +0.0017 for fixed 12bps over days 10-60; also loses on TEST (b_walkforward.json)
Timed exits at +2/+3/+4 min: second taker fee + 1c spread make all of them dominated by hold-to-close (model sim n=955 and empirical pm p150 check n=49 agree; d_exits.json)
Low thresholds 6-8bps: q_TRAIN approximately 0.519 with p-vs-breakeven approximately 0.9; sub-1c TEST edge at 51c; never clears fees anywhere
25bps threshold: TRAIN q=0.5105 (worse than 12bps), n collapses to approximately 1000 full-period; no improvement over 20bps

Treating the 10d 56% as the long-run parameter: 60d truth is 53.3% at 12bps; days 10-30 were flat-to-inverted (window q 0.477 and 0.507), so always-on sizing based on 56% overstates edge approximately 2x
Entries in the 53-55c band (live revEntryMax=0.55): EV negative at TEST q for 12bps signals; the cap should be 53c (54c only for 20+bps signals, whose TRAIN behavior inverts anyway)
Upweighting 20+bps moves for their higher TEST q (0.5648): the size gradient does not exist on TRAIN (0.518 < 0.526 for 12-20bps) — TEST-only pattern, not actionable
rejectedTitles: 60d truth: reversal direction persists but at 53.3%, not 56%; edge is non-stationary and fails the 51c fee hurdle on TRAIN
Walk-forward threshold selection is worse than fixed 12bps — the parameter is not improvable by trailing calibration
Entry-price truth: contrarian side costs a median 52.5c at t0+20s, and EV turns negative above 53c — the live 55c cap is 2c too loose
Timed exits at +2/+3/+4 min are fee-dead; hold-to-close dominates
BEST SPEC — fixed 12bps contrarian, hold-to-close, cap 53c, no adaptation: clears fees on TEST (+2.6c/share) but is regime-conditional

dead: FV-threshold stop-loss overlay, all 24 swept configs (X 0.10-0.45, fire-from 60/120/180s): TRAIN-best +$1,881 flips to -$849 on TEST, boot p=0.25; sd drops 13% but maxDD worsens — not even good variance control (work/exits/sim_results.json)
Opposite-side hedge overlay: numerically identical to selling out at every config (within $3); double fee + spread makes it strictly dominated by doing nothing (work/exits/sim_results.json)
Stops/hedges on the reversal-family book specifically (n=155): every config sign-flips between TRAIN and TEST, worst case TRAIN +$47 -> TEST -$1,529; the live +EV book must be held to resolution

'Recoverable loser' salvage (65% of losers touched fv>=0.5 post-entry, median salvage 95% of cost): pure hindsight max — any implementable trigger that captures it fires on winners at rates that erase the gain (work/exits/autopsy.json)
Buy-the-favorite at 20s (the book's actual entry timing): 50.9% win at 54.3c fill, -5.1c/share after fees — informationless; independently corroborates 'momentum is fee-dead'
Buy-the-favorite at 150s: 73.9% win vs 75.4% breakeven, -1.3c/share — information fully priced by 150s
Ledger entrySec-bucket conditioning within momentum engines (n=2,046): non-monotone (-5.7/-0.7/-7.1/+2.1 $/trade), guard-timing confounded — no usable timing signal from the ledger itself
rejectedTitles: Adverse-excursion autopsy: losers look recoverable in hindsight, but there is no exploitable asymmetry at any trigger
Stop-loss overlay (sell when modeled Up price < X): TRAIN-best gain of +$1,881 sign-flips to -$849 on TEST
Opposite-side hedge is arithmetically identical to selling out — same failure, plus a second gas fee
Entry timing: the book's actual ~24s entries are informationless; waiting to 60s helps by +8.9c/share paired but the effect decays and misses the bar
Mechanism: the market over-discounts early adverse excursions — dips at 60s still win 35-40%, which is why every exit overlay loses here

design.summary: Best Model v2 designed and written to DESIGN.md. Kills 7 of 9 engines (loose/floor/band/strict/value/fade/reversal2 — all momentum/value/fee-dead or dead code), keeps a two-arm reversal family: flagship latentfire_v2 (12bps contrarian, 53c cap, first-45s entry, hold-to-close, loop-managed eff12 gate launched at 0.48 with effMax=1.0 as the legal gate-off state) plus ungated reversal_v2 as a live 50/50 control arm. Every claim is tagged CONFIRMED/PRIOR/CANDIDATE/DEAD; the only confirmed empirical input is the fill model (wtd fill .486, hurdle q*=.5036, ~31-45% of signals modeled and executed as skips), and the edge itself is quoted honestly at +1.9c/share point with a CI spanning negative. Includes the requested perpetual learning loop (nightly walk-forward with frozen-vs-refit tables, 250-signal minimums, ±0.05/night step caps, 5-day/150-signal shadow promotion, asymmetric retirement/revival hysteresis, terminal kills for momentum, and regret-vs-frozen-baseline self-audit), quarter-Kelly fraction-of-bank sizing with shrunk qhat (no daily loss/trade caps — verified dominated), risk controls (no stops/hedges, real-book-only, Coinbase-only trigger, feed-outage stand-down), and a pre-registered 60-day validation plan with power math (~2,200 fills to resolve q=.53 vs hurdle) and explicit SUCCESS/FAIL/AMBIGUOUS thresholds.
decisions: Kill all 6 momentum/value/fade engines permanently (fee-death is structural, not parametric — they are barred from the revival mechanism) and kill reversal2 (gamma fallback fired 0/67, entries a strict subset of reversal)

Tighten revEntryMax 0.55 -> 0.53: justified by fee arithmetic alone (q*(0.55)=.5673 exceeds every measured q estimate; even prior q=.56 loses at 55c), costing availability ~69% -> ~55% but dropping only -EV fills; the ~31-45% of signals above cap are modeled and executed as SKIPS per the confirmed fill model

Keep the efficiency-gate concept (validated 10d prior) but make its constant the learning loop's primary refit target with effMax=1.0 as a legal gate-off state — resolving the 10d-validated vs 60d-stale conflict empirically instead of by fiat, with the ungated arm live at 50/50 as control

Freeze everything the research verified as un-adaptable: 12bps trigger (walk-forward reselection -0.44c vs +0.17c/share), hold-to-close (all exits fee-dead/sign-flipping), entry timing, slip, fee model, buffered construction

Perpetual loop is parameter-maintenance only, never structure invention: 250-signal refit minimums, ±0.05/night step caps, shadow-before-promotion, max 1 promotion/engine/week, auto-rollback, asymmetric retire/revive hysteresis, and a permanent frozen-baseline shadow whose regret judges the loop itself (freeze refits if regret < -$50 at day 30)

Sizing replaces flat $50 (=0.73x full Kelly, verified too hot) with quarter-Kelly fraction-of-bank on shrunk qhat; NO daily loss/trade caps (EV cost ~2x DD reduction at every level) — drawdown control is stake fraction only, plus a $250 catastrophic ops stop
Base-rate guard: if trailing-15d ungated net/share < -1c, shrink qhat toward 0.5 (sizing down) rather than tightening gates — the only verified-dominant response to regime death

Pre-registered validation: 60 days, ~1,500-2,000 fills (resolves edges >= ~2.5-3pp; power math stated), SUCCESS = net >= +1c/share with 90% block-boot CI > 0, FAIL = <= -0.5c or CI upper < +0.5c, one 90-day extension max with ambiguity treated as FAIL for scaling; monthly re-audit of the confirmed fill model
Candidate-tagged items (eff6/cnt12 impulse gate, 20bps trigger) run as zero-stake shadows only and can never exceed half stake without a human re-verification round

critique.overall: This is one of the more honest designs I have red-teamed: the kill list is evidence-backed, the cost model matches the brief exactly (all five q* break-evens verify), the fill-model numbers match the artifacts (fill_dist.json, VERDICT.json), and the document repeatedly refuses to claim more edge than +1-3c unconfirmed. The frozen/refit split, skip-as-decision logging, and regret_vs_frozen baseline are genuinely good guardrails. But it has one outright bug (the qhat shrinkage formula's prior mean is 0.25, not 0.5 — as written the sizing engine believes the strategy is a disaster and trades $5 floors anyway), one self-contradiction created by its own cap change (fill-conformance anchors are the 55c-cap quantiles), and one taxonomy violation (the eff12<=0.48 gate sign-flips TRAIN->TEST in the 60d data per stream_stats.json — the exact pattern used to execute every other overlay — yet the gated arm is the FLAGSHIP). The learning loop is the weakest section: its promotion (+0.5c at ~100 fills, ~0.1 SE) and rollback bars sit far below statistical resolution, and its nightly cadence contradicts the cited deploy_spec evidence that even 5-day refits thrash; expect a bounded random walk in effMax that the day-30 regret freeze eventually halts. The live gated arm is informationally redundant (deterministic gate + stake-independent paper fills = the shadow book answers the same question free). Finally, the pre-registered Phase 2 has only ~54% power at the design's own +1.9c point estimate, so a true edge of exactly the size claimed more likely than not ends AMBIGUOUS->FAIL — acceptable conservatism, but it must be stated. Fix the six mustFix items and this is a defensible pre-registration; ship it as written and the loop plus the sizing bug will generate losses and churn that have nothing to do with whether the reversal edge is real."
mustFix: QHAT SHRINKAGE FORMULA IS ARITHMETICALLY WRONG (DESIGN.md 3.1): `qhat=(wins+200*0.5)/(n+400)` has prior mass 100 in the numerator but 400 in the denominator, so the prior mean is 0.25, not 0.5. At launch (n=0) qhat=0.25; even after 900 settled fills at 52% it only reaches 0.437 — permanently below every break-even q*. Combined with `clamp(..., $5, ...)`, the $5 floor forces trades the estimator itself scores at roughly -25c/share. Fix: numerator prior = 400*0.5 = 200 (verified: (wins+200)/(n+400) gives 0.500/0.504/0.510 at n=0/100/400), and make f_full<=0 a SKIP, not a $5-floored stake.

FILL-CONFORMANCE ANCHORS CONTRADICT THE DESIGN'S OWN CAP CHANGE (3.5.3 vs 2): the alert anchors p25/50/75 = .47/.51/.53 are the 55c-cap ledger quantiles, but v2 caps effective cost at 53c. Recomputing the same 155-trade ledger censored at <=.53 (n=123, 79% retained) gives p25/50/75 = .450/.490/.510, wtd mean .4774. Under the new cap a HEALTHY book trips the |dp50|>1.5c alarm (~2c mechanical shift), and real richening drift gets normalized against it. Recompute anchors under the 53c cap before day 1 (data: scratchpad/data/trades.json; script: work/microstructure/ledger_fill.py).

LOOP DECISION THRESHOLDS ARE 5-10x BELOW STATISTICAL RESOLUTION (3.2): per-share settled-outcome SD is ~50c, so SE(net/share) at the promotion minimum (150 signals, ~80-120 fills) is ~4-5c; the +0.5c promotion bar is ~0.1 SE — promotions are coin flips, and the 10-day auto-rollback bar (1c at ~250-350 fills, SE ~2.6-3c) can't reliably catch a bad one. Nightly effMax refit with |step|<=0.05 in [0.30,1.00] will execute a bounded random walk. Worse, the cadence contradicts its own citation: work/regime/deploy_spec.json specifies refit_every_days=10 and documents that 5-day refits are WORSE than 10-day ('refit thrash') — nightly is 10x faster than the evidence supports. Fix: refit every 10 days per the spec, and state promotion/rollback bars in bootstrap-CI terms (e.g., shadow-minus-incumbent CI lower bound > 0), accepting that most windows will resolve to 'no change'.

THE GATE'S 60d EVIDENCE IS A TRAIN->TEST SIGN FLIP, THE DESIGN'S OWN DEATH CRITERION (1, 2): work/sizing/stream_stats.json shows TRAIN gated (eff12<=0.48) q=.5182 vs TRAIN all .5227 vs TRAIN ungated-hi-eff .5386 — on TRAIN the gate selected WORSE trades; only TEST shows the help (.5629 vs .5281). The design kills every other overlay for exactly this pattern ('sign-flips TRAIN->TEST') and the brief's bar requires TRAIN persistence, yet the gated arm is named FLAGSHIP and effMax is the loop's primary refit target. Relabel: reversal_v2 is the flagship, latentfire_v2 is the experiment; this also changes what 'regret' and the day-60 gate verdict are benchmarked against.

CITATION ERROR ON THE NO-CAPS JUSTIFICATION (4): the design quotes 'L150: -7.0% EV / -3.8% DD; N3: -57% / -24%' as percentages, but work/sizing/caps_livefreq2_results.json shows these are DOLLAR figures (L150 ev_cost $6.96 / dd_red $3.84 on mean $345.8, i.e. 2.0%/0.9%; N3 $57.2/$23.8). The qualitative conclusion (EV cost ~= 2x DD reduction) survives, but the record must quote the artifact correctly — this document is the pre-registration.

DOUBLE-STAKE CONCENTRATION ON GATED INTERVALS IS UNSPECIFIED (4): on choppy intervals both live arms fire on the identical side at the identical fill, so if each arm sizes quarter-Kelly independently the family runs HALF-Kelly exactly on the gated subset — the fraction the sizing study rejected (kelly_h: 42% median DD vs 23%). The combined <=5%-of-bank cap only binds at the extreme. Specify the rule explicitly: per-arm quarter-Kelly on its half-bank allocation, or a family-level Kelly with a 50/50 attribution split.

final.summary: FINAL-DESIGN.md (v2.1) rewrites DESIGN.md with every red-team mustFix fixed (all six verified correct against the artifacts, none rebutted), all four shouldCuts accepted, and all seven missing items closed with fresh computations. Headline changes: the qhat shrinkage bug is fixed to (wins+200)/(n+400) with f_full<=0 now a SKIP (no $5 floor); fill-conformance anchors are re-derived under the 53c cap (p25/50/75 = .450/.490/.510, n=123, unweighted mean .4774 / share-wtd .4724); the ungated reversal_v2 becomes the sole live flagship while the eff12-gated latentfire_v2 is demoted to a zero-stake shadow (per its TRAIN->TEST sign flip in stream_stats.json), which also structurally resolves the double-Kelly concentration issue; the learning loop drops nightly refits for the deploy_spec's 10-day cadence with paired block-bootstrap-CI promotion bars (>=400 paired signals, 90% CI LB>0) and nightly work reduced to metrics/asserts/qhat; the caps citation is corrected to dollars ($6.96 EV vs $3.84 DD on mean $345.8). New machinery: a stake-independent measure_book that is both the Phase-2 instrument and the qhat data source (preventing estimator starvation under the new skip rule); a three-tier base-rate guard whose latencies were quantified by simulation against the real 60d dead window (tier-1 fires day 17.1 vs onset ~day 10; new 7d provisional tier fires day 13.7; new hard-bench tier cuts injected q=.38 disaster peak-DD from 63.5% to 31.8%); nightly fee-formula and market-metadata asserts (feasibility verified: max fee diff 5e-6 across 3,143 ledger fills); a pm-resolution-outage rule that skips qhat/refits rather than proxy-filling; missed_open_trade skip logging; and Phase-0 pre-registration of availability (unknown between 55% and 79%) with a power re-derivation rule. Phase-2 power at the design's own q=.523 point estimate is now stated explicitly: 73-92% at day-60 n under the 53c-cap fill mix (hurdle .490-.495), versus only 37-55% under the old mix — with the honest admission that the cap change is what makes the pre-registration adequately powered. Verification artifacts saved under work/final-design/ (checks.py/.json, guard_stress, guard_disaster, guard_bench).
changes: Fixed qhat shrinkage to (wins+200)/(n+400) (prior mean now 0.5; verified .500/.504/.510 at n=0/100/400 @52%) and made f_full<=0 a SKIP with the $5 stake floor removed (MF1)

Re-derived fill-conformance anchors under the 53c cap from the same 155-trade ledger: p25/50/75 = .450/.490/.510, n=123 (79.4% retained), unweighted mean .4774, share-weighted .4724 (MF2; work/final-design/checks.json)

Moved all parameter refits to the deploy_spec 10-day cadence and restated promotion/rollback as paired 1h-block-bootstrap CI conditions (>=10d, >=400 paired signals, CI lower bound > 0), declaring 'no change' the expected modal outcome; nightly job reduced to metrics/asserts/qhat only (MF3, SC2)

Relabeled reversal_v2 (ungated) as the flagship and only live arm; demoted latentfire_v2 to a zero-stake shadow per the eff12-gate TRAIN->TEST sign flip in stream_stats.json (TRAIN gated .5182 < all .5227 < hi-eff .5386); regret, guard, and the day-60 gate verdict re-benchmarked accordingly (MF4, SC1)
Corrected the caps citation to dollar figures with derived percentages: L150 $6.96 EV (2.0%) vs $3.84 medDD (0.9%); N3 $57.24 (16.6%) vs $23.80 (5.8%) (MF5)

Specified the concentration rule: single live arm at launch; any future second live arm sizes quarter-Kelly on a half-bank allocation with a family per-interval cap of 5% of bank enforced in eval (MF6)
Initialized impulse_shadow at the deploy-calibrated A=0.10/B=6 (not stale TRAIN A=0.32) and dropped step caps for zero-stake shadows (SC3)
Cut the .554 cherry-picked row; EV table is now two rows (q=.523 measured, q=.380 disaster) across both fill mixes (SC4)

Added the measure_book: a stake-independent record of every cap-compliant fill that serves as the Phase-1/2 measurement instrument and the qhat data source, preventing estimator starvation from the new skip rule (sized book starts at ~43% of cap-compliant fills at launch qhat .5063)

Stated Phase-2 power at the design's own point estimate with a bracketed block-bootstrap inflation: 73-92% at day-60 n under the 53c mix vs 37-55% under the old mix, plus a pre-registered Phase-0 power re-derivation rule extending to day 90 if realized fills come in rich (M1)

Added nightly integrity asserts: exact fee-per-fill check (max historical diff 5e-6 over 3,143 fills) and market-metadata hash (resolution source, tie rule, series slug/cadence); any diff freezes entries (M2)

Quantified base-rate-guard latency by simulation against the real 60d dead window and an injected 20d q=.38 episode; added a 7d provisional tier (latency 7.1d -> 3.7d) and a hard-bench tier (disaster peak-DD 63.5% -> 31.8%), both declared ops controls with logged counterfactuals (M3; guard_stress/guard_disaster/guard_bench.json)
Added missed_open_trade as a first-class skip reason with a stated settlement-lag assumption measured in Phase 0 (M4)
Added the pm-resolution outage rule: skip qhat updates and refit bookkeeping, never substitute the Coinbase proxy; 3 consecutive outage nights freeze entries (M5)
Updated eval pseudocode with the one-sided-book re-poll loop, the cross-arm exposure check, and the ivlHist persistence/cold-start-rebuild rule (persisted at bot lines 358/936/1020) (M6)
Pre-registered availability under the 53c cap as a Phase-0 measured output (55.1% pm-sample vs 79.4% censored-ledger bracket) with the skip-alarm band re-derived from the measurement (M7)
